observations.recommendation: b. We further recommended and Management affirmed that the City Accountant restate the value of Land to its revalued amounts and recognize revaluation increases and decreases in the books after the revaluation appraisal/revaluation of land by the GSO and City Assessor.
observations.managementAction: Management affirmed that the City Accountant has not revaluation has been made by the GSO and City Assessor. Consequently, no increases or decreases were recognized in the books.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the City Accountant, in close collaboration with the
General Services Officer, agreed to prioritize and expedite the submission of
a Request for Authority to Derecognize Non-Existing PPEs, in accordance
with applicable regulations to provide a more accurate financial picture,
prevent asset overstatement and improve the reliability of financial
reporting.

We recommended and the City Accountant, City Engineer and General
Services Officer conformed to maintain ongoing record updates based on
RPCPPE inventory results and perform the necessary reconciliation between
property and accounting records, following established COA guidelines and
procedures.

We recommended and the City Accountant agreed to provide the City
Treasurer with the required documentation to facilitate necessary cashbook
adjustments. Subsequently, the City Treasurer concurred to immediately
reconcile and correct these adjustments within their cashbook.

We further recommended and the City Accountant and City Treasurer
conformed to regularly coordinate as regards the Cash accounts, including
reconciliation of records, so that errors can be resolved promptly in order to
present reliable Cash – Local Treasury and Cash in Bank balances.

We recommended and the City Accountant agreed to undertake an
immediate and thorough review of all dormant accounts within the financial
statements. If any account is deemed non-existent or unsubstantiated, the
City Accountant should initiate the necessary procedures for its disposition,
adhering strictly to the guidelines stipulated in the relevant COA Circular.

Furthermore, we recommended and the City Accountant acknowledged to
implement a system for regular account reconciliation and monitoring to
prevent the accumulation of dormant and undocumented accounts in the
future.

We recommended and the City Accountant concurred to adjust the relevant
balances of accounts to reflect the correct amount of reciprocal transactions.

We also recommended and it was agreed by the City Accountant to enforce
a policy requiring the synchronous recording of all intra-agency reciprocal
transactions such as implementing internal controls and regular
reconciliation procedures to detect and prevent asynchronous recording.

We recommended and the CHRMO assented to immediately intensify and
expedite the hiring or promotion of qualified personnel to fill all vacant
plantilla positions. This action will enhance operational efficiency, ensure
compliance with relevant regulations, and reduce the City's reliance on
JO/COS workers, thereby strengthening the stability and quality of public
services.
a. We recommended that the Inventory Committee undertake and complete the physical count of the PPE accounts.
observations.managementAction: Management agreed

observations.recommendation: b. We also recommended and Management concurred that the City General Services Officer and the City Accountant continue the updating of their records based on the partial result of the inventory count per RPCPPE and conduct the required reconciliation of the property records with the accounting records in accordance with the guidelines and procedures outlined in COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.
observations.managementAction: Management concurred that the City General Services Officer and the City Accountant continue the updating of their records based on the partial result of the inventory count per RPCPPE and conduct the required reconciliation of the property records with the accounting records in accordance with the guidelines and procedures outlined in COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.

observations.recommendation: a. We recommended that the General Services Officer and City Assessor exert collaborative efforts in reconciling the market value of at least 10% of land reported and conducting an appraisal/revaluation of Land as required under existing regulations.
observations.managementAction: Management agreed that the General Services Officer and City Assessor exert collaborative efforts in reconciling the market value of at least 10% of land reported and conducting an appraisal/revaluation of Land as required under existing regulations.

observations.recommendation: a. We recommended that the City Accountant in coordination with the City Treasurer return the unspent balances of fund transfers to the Source Agencies after confirming that the purpose of the fund transfer agreed in the MOA has already been completed.
observations.managementAction: Management assented that the City Accountant in coordination with the City Treasurer return the unspent balances of fund transfers to the Source Agencies after confirming that the purpose of the fund transfer agreed in the MOA has already been completed.

observations.recommendation: b. We also recommended that the City Accountant request for the write-off on the balances of fund transfers recorded in the Due to LGUs account under the General and Trust Funds without specific details or the Source Agencies cannot be traced anymore following the guidelines and procedures under COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.
observations.managementAction: Management conformed that the City Accountant request for the write-off on the balances of fund transfers recorded in the Due to LGUs account under the General and Trust Funds without specific details or the Source Agencies cannot be traced anymore following the guidelines and procedures under COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

observations.recommendation: c. Hereafter, we recommended and it was agreed by Management that the City Accountant regularly monitor the receipts and utilization of fund transfers from other LGUs under the Due to LGUs account and any unspent amount after implementation shall be returned to the Source Agency depending on condition in the MOA.
observations.managementAction: Management agreed that the City Accountant regularly monitor the receipts and utilization of fund transfers from other LGUs under the Due to LGUs account and any unspent amount after implementation shall be returned to the Source Agency depending on condition in the MOA.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the General Services Office facilitate and expedite the processing of at least 50 lots to establish absolute ownership and protect the City's legal interests in these real properties.
observations.managementAction: Management agreed that the General Services Office facilitate and expedite the processing of at least 50 lots to establish absolute ownership and protect the City's legal interests in these real properties.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Treasurer properly assess the amount of collections handled by each AO and ensure that all their accountabilities are sufficiently covered by the fidelity bond and in case the cash accountability increases, require the AO to apply for an increase in the amount of fidelity bond.
observations.managementAction: Management agreed that the City Treasurer properly assess the amount of collections handled by each AO and ensure that all their accountabilities are sufficiently covered by the fidelity bond and in case the cash accountability increases, require the AO to apply for an increase in the amount of fidelity bond.

observations.recommendation: a. We recommended that the City Accountant, City Budget Officer submit a plan on how to settle the various obligations without the corresponding cash back-up or how these obligations will be met.
observations.managementAction: Management agreed that the City Accountant and City Budget Officer submit a plan on how to settle the various obligations without the corresponding cash back-up or how these obligations will be met.

observations.recommendation: b. Furthermore, we recommended and Management concurred that the City Accountant, City Budget Officer, and City Treasurer closely coordinate and observe the limitations in the disbursement of government funds pursuant to Section 337 of RA No. 7160 to ensure that no cash overdraft in any government fund will be incurred at any given time of the year.
observations.managementAction: Management concurred that the City Accountant, City Budget Officer, and City Treasurer closely coordinate and observe the limitations in the disbursement of government funds pursuant to Section 337 of RA No. 7160 to ensure that no cash overdraft in any government fund will be incurred at any given time of the year.

observations.recommendation: c. Finally, we recommended that austerity measures be adopted by Management to avoid incurrence of cash overdraft in its future operations.
observations.managementAction: Management assented to our recommendation that austerity measures be adopted by Management to avoid incurrence of cash overdraft in its future operations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed that the City Accountant and the City General Services Officer undertake the necessary reconciliation of property records following the guidelines and procedures in COA Circular No. 2020-006, update subsidiary ledgers, and secure the certified list that must be included as part of the PPE for recognition in the books.
observations.managementAction: As to PPE records from 2017 to earlier years, reconciliation is on-going, while those from 2018 to present are updated and reconciled with the office of the accounting.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed that the GSO fast-track the physical inventory count of PPEs with existing/unidentified records and reconcile the data with the City Accountant to come up with the final list of non-existing/unidentified PPEs for derecognition. Also, for the City Mayor to file a Request for Authority to Derecognize non-existing/missing PPEs pursuant to Section 8.2 of COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 30, 2020.
observations.managementAction: Out of 48 offices, 27 were completed the physical inventory, 12 offices partially completed and the remaining 9 are scheduled for next conduct of inventory.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed that the GSO facilitate and supervise the processing of the TCTs of untitled lots to establish absolute ownership and protect the City's legal interests in these real properties.
observations.managementAction: Thirty-nine lots have been transferred in favor of the city government of Bago and presently at the Register of Deeds for the release of titles.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to transfer the City’s depository accounts from the PVB – Bacolod branch to LBP – Bago Branch in compliance with the provisions of the DOF Department Circular to deposit and maintain accounts with LBP. Only maintain a deposit with PVB up to the extent of their contractual commitment with the said bank.
observations.managementAction: Regular deposit collections, encashment of checks issued to individual payees are now withdrawn from GFIs with a universal license and a CAMELS rating of at least 3, and plan, eventual transfer of NTA from PVB to LBP.

observations.recommendation:
1.8 We recommended and Management agreed that the Inventory Committee
undertake and complete the physical count of the PPE accounts.
1.9 We also recommended and Management concurred that the City General
Services Officer and the City Accountant continue the updating of their
records based on the partial result of the inventory count per RPCPPE and
conduct the required reconciliation of the property records with the
accounting records in accordance with the guidelines and procedures
outlined in COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.

We recommended and Management agreed that the City Accountant validate the list of unserviceable vehicles and motorcycles for disposal from the GSO, and the General Services Officer undertake the immediate disposal of unserviceable properties. After disposal the City Accountant must prepare appropriate adjusting entries to derecognize or drop said unserviceable items from the books.
observations.managementAction: The City Accountant validate the list of unserviceable vehicles and motorcycles for disposal from the GSO, and the General Services Officer undertake the immediate disposal of unserviceable properties. After disposal the City Accountant must prepare appropriate adjusting entries to derecognize or drop said unserviceable items from the books.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed that the City through the BAC ascertain that all its procurement activities shall undergo the procurement process via competitive public bidding or in cases where public bidding is not feasible, employ alternative modes of procurement as long as the conditions outlined in the Revised IRR are strictly followed to ensure transparency in the procurement process and extend equal opportunity to eligible and qualified suppliers to participate in the procurement.
observations.managementAction: The City through the BAC ascertain that all its procurement activities shall undergo the procurement process via competitive public bidding or in cases where public bidding is not feasible, employ alternative modes of procurement as long as the conditions outlined in the Revised IRR are strictly followed to ensure transparency in the procurement process and extend equal opportunity to eligible and qualified suppliers to participate in the procurement.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed that henceforth, the CSWDO, COVID-19 IMT, and other implementing offices of the City’s programs, projects, and activities (PPAs) wherein meals are served to ascertain that the allocated amount for meals is in proportion to the number of patients/participants.
observations.managementAction: Henceforth, the CSWDO, COVID-19 IMT, and other implementing offices of the City’s programs, projects, and activities (PPAs) wherein meals are served to ascertain that the allocated amount for meals is in proportion to the number of patients/participants.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend and Management agreed that the City Mayor:
a) Direct the Implementing Office or In-charge in the implementation of the fund transfer to fast-track the implementation of the programs or projects covered by the Memorandum of Agreement;
b) Require the City Treasurer in coordination with the City Accountant to return the unspent balances of fund transfers to the Source Agency after completion of the projects/programs or as agreed in the MOA; and
c) Instruct the City Accountant to revert to the General Fund the balances of fund transfers with details that cannot be traced anymore in consonance with COA Circular No. 94-013 so it may be used for other purposes to the advantage of the City.
observations.managementAction: a) Direct the Implementing Office or In-charge in the implementation of the fund transfer to fast-track the implementation of the programs or projects covered by the Memorandum of Agreement; b) Require the City Treasurer in coordination with the City Accountant to return the unspent balances of fund transfers to the Source Agency after completion of the projects/programs or as agreed in the MOA; and c) Instruct the City Accountant to revert to the General Fund the balances of fund transfers with details that cannot be traced anymore in consonance with COA Circular No. 94-013 so it may be used for other purposes to the advantage of the City.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Accountant and GSO officer undertake the required reconciliation of property records pursuant to Section 114 of Manual on NGAS, Volume I and effect the necessary adjustments to fairly present the said accounts in the financial statement. Furthermore, investigate the noted differences and if found that these properties are missing, hold the accountable officer liable thereof.

We recommended that the GSO officer and City Accountant fast track the corroboration of data of PPE accounts under verification and submit a Request for Authority to Derecognize Non-Existing PPEs as required under Section 8.0 of COA Circular No. 2020-006.

We recommended the City Mayor create a Disposal Committee who shall coordinate with the Inventory Team and the Office of the City Accountant to:
Record obsolete properties with their corresponding acquisition costs in the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property for proper disposal and their consequent dropping from the books by the City Accountant and
Assess/estimate the values of properties without details as to acquisition costs and/or records of similar properties acquired during the same period.

We recommended that the City Engineer issue and timely submit Certificates of Completion on completed projects to the City Accountant for appropriate reclassification of the CIP accounts to the proper PPE accounts.
Moreover, we recommended that the City Accountant reclassify all completed projects in the CIP account to the appropriate asset accounts at                  year-end.  Henceforth, we also recommended that the City Accountant take up the corresponding depreciation expenses of the classified completed projects from the time of completion.

We recommended that the LVOC Nurse Coordinator furnish the City Accountant with a detailed list of allocated vaccines received by the City and for the City Accountant to recognize in the books the donations in accordance with existing regulations.
For donations received without costs, the City Accountant shall recognize the donations at their fair value as at the date of receipt, which may be ascertained by reference to an active market, or by appraisal as provided in Paragraph 97 of the IPSAS No. 23.
We recommended that the LVOC Nurse Coordinator monitor and prioritize administration of vaccines with earliest expiry date to ensure                               non-wastage of government resources.
We recommended that the City government exert effort to promote vaccine confidence in order to encourage more of its populace to be vaccinated in order to attain the herd immunity.

We recommended that the City Accountant adjust the Due to LGUs account under the Trust Fund to Prior Period Adjustments to recognize the income that should have been received by the City.
Subsequent receipt of tuition fees shall also be recognized under the correct account as set forth in the GAM for LGUs.
We recommended that the City Accountant deposit immediately the balance of PDAF to the Bureau of Treasury and secure proof of the return thereof.

We recommended that Management fast track the implementation of the fund transfers with available records, or if such cannot anymore be implemented, return the unspent balance to the source LGU or NGA.
We further recommended that the City Accountant revert to the General Fund the balances of fund transfers so it may be used for other purposes for the benefit of the City.

We recommended and the Accounting Office agreed to reconcile the monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements with the balances in the trial balance and record/adjust necessary reconciling items so that the accuracy and reliability of the Cash-in-Bank account can be validated.

We recommended and the GSO and its Inventory Committee also agreed to conduct periodic physical count of supplies and materials and submit the Report of Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) in accordance with the guidelines above.

Furthermore, we recommended and the Accounting Office agreed to prepare the necessary adjusting entry to record the Inventories to the proper Expense account or to Prior Years’ Adjustment to fairly present the amount in the financial statements.

We recommended and the Accounting Office and GSO agreed to undertake the required reconciliation of property records pursuant to Section 114 of Manual on NGAS, Volume I and effect the necessary adjustments to fairly present the said accounts in the financial statement.

Furthermore, we recommended the Accounting Office and GSO agreed to investigate the noted differences and if found that these properties are missing, hold the accountable officer liable thereof.
observations.reason: The GSO and Accounting Office is still in the process of reconciling the PPE accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor through the GSO file a Request for Authority to Derecognize Non-Existing PPEs with its supporting documents as required under Section 8.0 of COA Circular No. 2020-006 for the PPEs to be derecognized under the books of accounts.
observations.reason: The City Accountant has already started to derecognize PPEs with records against Government Equity in the amount of P137,907.06

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the GSO-Designate agreed to record these obsolete properties in the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property for proper disposal after which they shall be dropped from the accounts.

Moreover, it was agreed that an Appraisal/Disposal Committee be created to assess the value of these properties and facilitate request to the COA Technical Team which will set the floor price for public auction sale.
observations.reason: Difficulty of processing of documents for sale and auction.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and both the GSO and Accounting Office agreed to exert effort to reconcile their records regarding disposed property and equipment. 

Moreover, we recommended that the Accounting Office make necessary adjustments in prior years’ income once the appropriate PPE accounts are closed, and shall record future proceeds from disposal of PPE as Gain or Loss on Sale of PPE instead of Miscellaneous Income.

We recommended and the City LDRRM Officer agreed to maximize the LDRRMF by fully implementing programs and projects spelled out in the LDRRMP in accordance with certain guidelines, especially with regards to COVID-19 in order to strengthen the capacity of the LGU to confront potential hazards and disasters resulting in avoidance or reduction of loss of lives and assets.

We recommended and the BAC Secretariat agreed to require duly complied Omnibus Sworn Statements from the supplier, and strictly require the submission of the OSS for subsequent Emergency Purchases in case the supplier fails to submit complete documentary requirements after the contract has been awarded.

We recommended and the Accounting Office agreed to reconcile the monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements with the balances in the trial balance and record/adjust necessary reconciling items so that the accuracy and reliability of the Cash-in-Bank account can be validated.

We recommended and the GSO and its Inventory Committee also agreed to conduct periodic physical count of supplies and materials and submit the Report of Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) in accordance with the guidelines above.

Furthermore, we recommended and the Accounting Office agreed to prepare the necessary adjusting entry to record the Inventories to the proper Expense account or to Prior Years’ Adjustment to fairly present the amount in the financial statements.

We recommended and the Accounting Office and GSO agreed to undertake the required reconciliation of property records pursuant to Section 114 of Manual on NGAS, Volume I and effect the necessary adjustments to fairly present the said accounts in the financial statement.

Furthermore, we recommended the Accounting Office and GSO agreed to investigate the noted differences and if found that these properties are missing, hold the accountable officer liable thereof.

We recommended that the City Mayor through the GSO file a Request for Authority to Derecognize Non-Existing PPEs with its supporting documents as required under Section 8.0 of COA Circular No. 2020-006 for the PPEs to be derecognized under the books of accounts.

We recommended and the GSO-Designate agreed to record these obsolete properties in the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property for proper disposal after which they shall be dropped from the accounts.

Moreover, it was agreed that an Appraisal/Disposal Committee be created to assess the value of these properties and facilitate request to the COA Technical Team which will set the floor price for public auction sale.

We recommended and both the GSO and Accounting Office agreed to exert effort to reconcile their records regarding disposed property and equipment.

Moreover, we recommended that the Accounting Office make necessary adjustments in prior years’ income once the appropriate PPE accounts are closed, and shall record future proceeds from disposal of PPE as Gain or Loss on Sale of PPE instead of Miscellaneous Income.

We recommended and the City LDRRM Officer agreed to maximize the LDRRMF by fully implementing programs and projects spelled out in the LDRRMP in accordance with certain guidelines, especially with regards to COVID-19 in order to strengthen the capacity of the LGU to confront potential hazards and disasters resulting in avoidance or reduction of loss of lives and assets.

We recommended and the BAC Secretariat agreed to require duly complied Omnibus Sworn Statements from the supplier, and strictly require the submission of the OSS for subsequent Emergency Purchases in case the supplier fails to submit complete documentary requirements after the contract has been awarded.

We recommended that the accounting office reconcile the monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements with the balances in the trial balance and record/adjust necessary reconciling items so that the accuracy and reliability of the Cash-in-Bank account can be validated.
observations.managementAction: Assign personnel dedicated to work on the reconciliation of cash in bank accounts to reflect general ledger balances.
observations.reason: Verification of checks issued in three banks is voluminous; lack of personnel to work exclusively on verification and reconciliation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the GSO timely submit the required RPCPPE and work hand in hand with Accounting Department to update and reconcile the related subsidiary records to have assurance that the Property, Plant and Equipment accounts totaling P1,359,561,846.68 are fairly presented in the balance sheet.

Moreover, the Accounting Office and GSO should undertake the required reconciliation of property records effect the necessary adjustments to fairly present the said accounts in the financial statement. Noted differences should also be investigated and if found that these properties are missing, hold the accountable officer liable thereof.

Henceforth, inventory taking and reconciliation of related subsidiary records should be regularly undertaken to ensure correctness and accuracy of property accounts as of financial statement date.
observations.managementAction: Updated the records for 2018 and 2019 PPEs.
observations.reason: Reconciliation of earlier years is still on-going.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Accountant prepare the adjustments manually for completed projects totaling P194,671,449.05 and effect the transfer of amount to appropriate PPE accounts. Further, compute the required depreciation on the completed projects and accordingly adjust the balances of the affected accounts.
observations.managementAction: On-going task
observations.reason: Person assigned with regular workload and the tasks called for in action plan can only be done during workdays with no workload.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management expedite the processing of the required supporting documents for the purchase of land amounting to P15,218,524.80, as mandated by Section 13.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 and submit the same to the Audit Team to facilitate evaluation of the contract and for other appropriate audit actions.

We recommended that the Supervising Environmental Management Specialist (SEMS) strictly implement the “no segregation, no collection policy” under RA No. 9003 among its residents to be closely monitored by the barangay officials. Moreover, each Solid Waste Management Committee created by the barangays should adopt their own solid waste management policies. In addition, monitoring and evaluation should be regularly done by the SEMS regarding the barangays’ policies to ensure the preservation and protection the environment as mandated by the Act.

We recommended that the concerned officials of the City submit the duly notarized Memorandum of Agreement between the City and beneficiaries in order to establish responsibility and accountability over the project and to ensure success in the implementation of the project.

We recommended that the City Mayor issue an Executive Order or Administrative Order creating the GAD Monitoring and Evaluation Team to oversee the implementation of the GAD system as required under Sections 3.4            and 5.1 of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01.

We recommended that the agency submit the distribution lists or acknowledgement receipts of the recipients of jetmatic pumps as basis for confirmation and in consonance with Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445.

We recommended that the accounting office reconcile the monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements with the balances in the trial balance and record/adjust necessary reconciling items so that the accuracy and reliability of the Cash-in-Bank account can be validated.

We recommended that the GSO timely submit the required RPCPPE and work hand in hand with Accounting Department to update and reconcile the related subsidiary records to have assurance that the Property, Plant and Equipment accounts totaling P1,359,561,846.68 are fairly presented in the balance sheet.

Moreover, the Accounting Office and GSO should undertake the required reconciliation of property records effect the necessary adjustments to fairly present the said accounts in the financial statement. Noted differences should also be investigated and if found that these properties are missing, hold the accountable officer liable thereof.

Henceforth, inventory taking and reconciliation of related subsidiary records should be regularly undertaken to ensure correctness and accuracy of property accounts as of financial statement date.

We recommended that the City Accountant prepare the adjustments manually for completed projects totaling P194,671,449.05 and effect the transfer of amount to appropriate PPE accounts. Further, compute the required depreciation on the completed projects and accordingly adjust the balances of the affected accounts.

We recommended that Management expedite the processing of the required supporting documents for the purchase of land amounting to P15,218,524.80, as mandated by Section 13.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 and submit the same to the Audit Team to facilitate evaluation of the contract and for other appropriate audit actions.

We recommended that the Supervising Environmental Management Specialist (SEMS) strictly implement the “no segregation, no collection policy” under RA No. 9003 among its residents to be closely monitored by the barangay officials. Moreover, each Solid Waste Management Committee created by the barangays should adopt their own solid waste management policies. In addition, monitoring and evaluation should be regularly done by the SEMS regarding the barangays’ policies to ensure the preservation and protection the environment as mandated by the Act.

We recommended that the concerned officials of the City submit the duly notarized Memorandum of Agreement between the City and beneficiaries in order to establish responsibility and accountability over the project and to ensure success in the implementation of the project.

We recommended that the City Mayor issue an Executive Order or Administrative Order creating the GAD Monitoring and Evaluation Team to oversee the implementation of the GAD system as required under Sections 3.4            and 5.1 of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01.

We recommended that the agency submit the distribution lists or acknowledgement receipts of the recipients of jetmatic pumps as basis for confirmation and in consonance with Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445.

We recommended that the GSO and Accounting Department work hand in hand to update and reconcile the related subsidiary records to have assurance that the Property, Plant and Equipment accounts totaling P989,615,089.86 are fairly presented in the balance sheet.

observations.managementAction: The agency will ensure control measures for the proper accounting of PPE acquisition and constantly coordinate to GSO in the on-going reconciliation process.
observations.reason: Two teams have been created for the inventory taking, but due to lack of personal and manpower the task was not possible for some PPE for SEF and TF specifically those purchased by the city for the schools.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that City Mayor and the City Budget Officer ensure that the appropriation and expenses for Personal Services are within the forty five percent (45%) limit as prescribed in Section 325(a) of R.A. No. 7160. Moreover, to be able to implement and provide additional PS costs, the City should make an effort in raising revenues otherwise, reduce PS expenses.

We recommended that the City Accountant prepare and submit the monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements for December 2018 pursuant to COA Circular No. 96-011 in order to ascertain the accuracy and reliability of the Cash-in-Bank, Local Currency, Current Account stated in the year-end financial statements.
observations.reason: The preparation of the monthly bank reconciliation statements was delayed because the 16 bank accounts with the depository banks are normally available 2 months after. The accounting office resorted for the request of snapshot for the month so that verification can be done.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Treasurer furnish the City Accountant a certified list showing the amount of taxes due and collectible at the beginning of the year as basis to record RPT/SET Receivable.

Moreover, we recommended that the City Accountant reconcile the RPT/SET Receivable accounts with the City Treasurer’s list of RPT delinquencies and make necessary adjustments to come up with a reconciled amount as mandated under Section 19 of the New Government Accounting System for Local Government Units.
We recommended that the Acting City Treasurer employ strategies to improve RPT collection efficiency as provided in Sections 256, 258 and 270 of RA No. 7160.

We recommended that the Acting City Treasurer intensify collection of Real Property Tax by developing strategies that will increase the collection of taxes.

We recommended further that the Acting City Treasurer lobby with the Department of Finance for the exclusion of properties owned by the defunct Energy Development Corporation in the computation of RPT target so that a more realizable amount will be reflected.

We recommended that the City Assessor prepare the required list of idle lands and submit to the City Treasurer to facilitate the computation and imposition of the additional ad valorem tax to generate additional income for the City Government.
We recommended that the City discontinue the procurement of accountable forms from private printing companies unless a waiver from the recognized government printers (RGPs) is first secured.
We recommended that the City Accountant reclassify the charges to Capital Outlay in compliance with Section 306 of the Local Government Code of 1991.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the Bids and Awards Committee and the City Accountant to submit the lacking supporting documents for review and evaluation and enforce strict compliance of the provisions of PD No. 1445, RA No. 9184 and COA Circular No. 2012-001.

We recommended that the City Accountant prepare the adjustments manually for completed projects totaling P280,558,966.15 that still remain with the CIP account and effect the transfer of amount to appropriate PPE accounts upon correction of the system error and compute for the required depreciation on the completed projects and accordingly adjust the balances of the affected amounts.
observations.reason: The task will involve a huge volume of additional work and will request management for authority to render overtime work to accomplish the job.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Agriculture/City Engineer submit the distribution lists or acknowledgment receipts of the recipients of fertilizers and hand pumps as basis for confirmation and in consonance with Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445.
We recommended that the City implement the projects funded by other government agencies as required in the Memorandum of Agreement to attain its purpose and thus, benefitting its constituents.
We recommended that the City Accountant require the Ayala Foundation to submit Utilization/Liquidation Report.

We recommended that an investigation be conducted regarding the circumstances behind the loss of 24 stubs of cash tickets with P5.00 denomination totaling P300,000.00 and file a case against the erring employee, if warranted.
observations.reason: Investigation was made and consequently filing of case by the City Legal Officer against the accountable officer which was approved by the Local Chief Executive.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the GSO and Accounting Department work hand in hand to update and reconcile the related subsidiary records to have assurance that the Property, Plant and Equipment accounts totaling P989,615,089.86 are fairly presented in the balance sheet.
Henceforth, inventory taking and reconciliation of related subsidiary records should be regularly undertaken to ensure correctness and accuracy of property accounts in the financial statement.

We recommended that City Mayor and the City Budget Officer ensure that the appropriation and expenses for Personal Services are within the forty five percent (45%) limit as prescribed in Section 325(a) of R.A. No. 7160. Moreover, to be able to implement and provide additional PS costs, the City should make an effort in raising revenues otherwise, reduce PS expenses.

We recommended that the City Accountant prepare and submit the monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements for December 2018 pursuant to COA Circular No. 96-011 in order to ascertain the accuracy and reliability of the Cash-in-Bank, Local Currency, Current Account stated in the year-end financial statements.

We recommended that the City Treasurer furnish the City Accountant a certified list showing the amount of taxes due and collectible at the beginning of the year as basis to record RPT/SET Receivable.
Moreover, we recommended that the City Accountant reconcile the RPT/SET Receivable accounts with the City Treasurer’s list of RPT delinquencies and make necessary adjustments to come up with a reconciled amount as mandated under Section 19 of the New Government Accounting System for Local Government Units.
We recommended that the Acting City Treasurer employ strategies to improve RPT collection efficiency as provided in Sections 256, 258 and 270 of RA No. 7160.

We recommended that the Acting City Treasurer intensify collection of Real Property Tax by developing strategies that will increase the collection of taxes.
We recommended further that the Acting City Treasurer lobby with the Department of Finance for the exclusion of properties owned by the defunct Energy Development Corporation in the computation of RPT target so that a more realizable amount will be reflected.

We recommended that the City Assessor prepare the required list of idle lands and submit to the City Treasurer to facilitate the computation and imposition of the additional ad valorem tax to generate additional income for the City Government.
We recommended that the City discontinue the procurement of accountable forms from private printing companies unless a waiver from the recognized government printers (RGPs) is first secured.

We recommended that the City Accountant reclassify the charges to Capital Outlay in compliance with Section 306 of the Local Government                     Code of 1991.
During our exit conference, Management replied that Item 2.1.3 of DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2014-155 dated December 17, 2014 specified the source of funds for the acquisition of motor vehicle.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the Bids and Awards Committee and the City Accountant to submit the lacking supporting documents for review and evaluation and enforce strict compliance of the provisions of PD No. 1445, RA No. 9184 and COA Circular No. 2012-001.

We recommended that the City Accountant prepare the adjustments manually for completed projects totaling P280,558,966.15 that still remain with the CIP account and effect the transfer of amount to appropriate PPE accounts upon correction of the system error and compute for the required depreciation on the completed projects and accordingly adjust the balances of the affected amounts.

We recommended that the City Agriculture/City Engineer submit the distribution lists or acknowledgment receipts of the recipients of fertilizers and hand pumps as basis for confirmation and in consonance with Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445.
We recommended that the City implement the projects funded by other government agencies as required in the Memorandum of Agreement to attain its purpose and thus, benefitting its constituents.

We recommended that the City Accountant require the Ayala Foundation to submit Utilization/Liquidation Report.
Management replied that they will coordinate with Ayala Foundation and the Department of Education and require the certificate of completion or liquidation if the project has already been completed.
It is recommended that a concrete plan be made to maximize the utilization of the SWM Fund through projects and programs that will benefit the constituents of the City.

It is recommended that a concrete plan be made to maximize the utilization the 1% LCPC fund through projects and programs that will benefit the constituents of the City in addition to the projects already implemented in CY 2018.
It is recommended that the City continue the implementation of such projects as envisioned by the program.
It is recommended to maximize the utilization of the LDRRMF through projects and programs that will benefit the constituents of the City.

We recommended that the GSO and Accounting Department work hand in hand to update and reconcile the related subsidiary records to have assurance that the PPE accounts totaling P1,257,201,430.03 are fairly presented in the balance sheet. 

Henceforth, inventory taking and reconciliation of related subsidiary records should be regularly undertaken to ensure correctness and accuracy of property accounts as of financial statement date.
observations.managementAction: Secured copies of PPEs at the accounting Office for reconciliation.
observations.reason: Updating and reconciliation of records are on-going

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Accounting Office draw up a journal entry voucher for properties which have exceeded their economic life reclassifying the total value of unserviceable properties from the PPE account to Other Assets account pending disposal.

We further recommended that the agency require the General Services Office to list down these obsolete properties in the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) for proper disposal. Likewise, an Appraisal/Disposal Committee shall be created to assess the value of these properties after which shall be submitted to the COA Technical and Information Technology Services (TITS) for review and inspection.
observations.reason: Identified PPE accounts for transfer but encountered problem as PPSAS no longer have Other Assets accounts in the Chart of Accounts

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Accounting Office manually compute for the depreciation of the affected government properties and adjustment should be made in the books. Henceforth, regular provision of depreciation for all government properties should be done to ensure that the assets of the City are fairly presented in the financial statement

We recommended that the City Accountant should book up RPT/SET Receivables at the start of the year based on RPTAR/Taxpayers Index Card as provided in Section 20 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume I so that the actual realizable RPT Income will be reflected in the financial reports.

We recommended that the City Treasurer release the share of RPT and other taxes due the barangays on a quarterly basis within five days after the end of each quarter as mandated by Section 271(d) of the Local Government Code in order to augment their meager income necessary to finance its developmental programs and projects for the benefit of their constituents.

Recommendations

Require the City Accountant to make necessary adjustment for the outstanding checks in the books in order to reflect a correct cash balance.

That the OIC-City Treasurer sends written notices to the payees of the stale checks.


We further recommended adherence to the procedure for the replacement of the stale checks whether it is still in the hands of the payee or unclaimed in accordance with the one prescribed for the handling and recording of the said checks.

We recommended that the prioritized programs and projects spelled out in the LDRRMP be fully implemented in order to strengthen the capacity of the LGU to confront potential hazards and disasters resulting in avoidance or reduction of loss of lives and assets.
observations.reason:
Lack of space to stockpile for disaster preparedness. Waiting for the DBM’s reply re: procurement of vehicles.
Lack of personnel to man the Early Warning Management.
Lack of office space due to delay of completion of the emergency operation center housing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor require the concerned offices to submit the revised AIP for CY 2017 covering the period CY 2012 STF as approved by the local sanggunian and LCE upon recommendation of the LDRRMC in accordance with the approved LDRRMP indicating the list of projects and activities including the subject expenditures to the Audit Team in compliance with JMC No. 2013-1, for further decisions in audit.

We recommended that the City maximize the appropriation and utilization of the 20% Development Fund allocated for the implementation of projects, programs and activities that are envisioned to help in the attainment of social, economic and environmental development in accordance with JMC 2017-1 dated                      February 22, 2017 of the DILG and DBM.
observations.reason: The schedule and the duration of the implementation of the PPAs are determined by the respective implementing department or office.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City give importance in the implementation of the projects funded by other government agencies as required in the Memorandum of Agreement so that the purpose for which these funds were granted are achieved for the benefit of its constituents.
We recommended that only expenditures listed as priority areas be charged to SEF pursuant to Section 272 and Section 100 (c) of the Local Government Code                (RA 7160).

Recommendation:
We recommended that the GSO and Accounting Department work hand in hand to update and reconcile the related subsidiary records to have assurance that the PPE accounts totaling P1,257,201,430.03 are fairly presented in the balance sheet.
Henceforth, inventory taking and reconciliation of related subsidiary records should be regularly undertaken to ensure correctness and accuracy of property accounts as of financial statement date.

Recommendations
We recommended that the Accounting Office draw up a journal entry voucher for properties which have exceeded their economic life reclassifying the total value of unserviceable properties from the PPE account to Other Assets account pending disposal.
We further recommended that the agency require the General Services Office to list down these obsolete properties in the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) for proper disposal. Likewise, an Appraisal/Disposal Committee shall be created to assess the value of these properties after which shall be submitted to the COA Technical and Information Technology Services (TITS) for review and inspection.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the City Accounting Office manually compute for the depreciation of the affected government properties and adjustment should be made in the books. Henceforth, regular provision of depreciation for all government properties should be done to ensure that the assets of the City are fairly presented in the financial statement.

Recommendation
We recommended that the City Accountant should book up RPT/SET Receivables at the start of the year based on RPTAR/Taxpayers Index Card as provided in Section 20 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume I so that the actual realizable RPT Income will be reflected in the financial reports.

Recommendations
We recommended that the City Treasurer release the share of RPT and other taxes due the barangays on a quarterly basis within five days after the end of each quarter as mandated by Section 271(d) of the Local Government Code in order to augment their meager income necessary to finance its developmental programs and projects for the benefit of their constituents.

Recommendation:
We recommended that an investigation be conducted regarding the circumstances behind the loss of 24 stubs of cash tickets with P5.00 denomination totaling P300,000.00 and file a case against the erring employee, if warranted.

Recommendations
Require the City Accountant to make necessary adjustment for the outstanding checks in the books in order to reflect a correct cash balance.
That the OIC-City Treasurer sends written notices to the payees of the stale checks.
We further recommended adherence to the procedure for the replacement of the stale checks whether it is still in the hands of the payee or unclaimed in accordance with the one prescribed for the handling and recording of the said checks.

Recommendation
We recommended that the prioritized programs and projects spelled out in the LDRRMP be fully implemented in order to strengthen the capacity of the LGU to confront potential hazards and disasters resulting in avoidance or reduction of loss of lives and assets.

Recommendation
We recommended that the City Mayor require the concerned offices to submit the revised AIP for CY 2017 covering the period CY 2012 STF as approved by the local sanggunian and LCE upon recommendation of the LDRRMC in accordance with the approved LDRRMP indicating the list of projects and activities including the subject expenditures to the Audit Team in compliance with JMC No. 2013-1, for further decisions in audit.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the City maximize the appropriation and utilization of the 20% Development Fund allocated for the implementation of projects, programs and activities that are envisioned to help in the attainment of social, economic and environmental development in accordance with JMC Circular 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017 of the DILG and DBM.

Recommendation
We recommended that the City give importance in the implementation of the projects funded by other government agencies as required in the Memorandum of Agreement so that the purpose for which these funds were granted are achieved for the benefit of its constituents.

Recommendation:
We recommended that only expenditures listed as priority areas be charged to SEF pursuant to Section 272 and Section 100 (c) of the Local Government Code (RA 7160).
We further recommended that the City Government strictly observe the provisions of Section 272 of the Local Government Code; Article 363 of its Implementing Rules and Regulations; and Joint Circular No. 01 s. 2017 dated January 19, 2017 of DepEd, DBM and DILG in the utilization of the SEF.

Recommendation
We recommended that the City maximize the utilization of its GAD budget so that its constituents could get the full benefits as envisioned by the program.
It is recommended that a concrete plan be made to maximize the utilization the 1% LCPC fund through projects and programs that will benefit the constituents of the City.

We recommended that the GSO and Accounting Department exert efforts to update and reconcile the property records of PPE with the PPE ledger cards to establish the correctness of PPE account balances amounting to P804,131,872.10, and for fair financial statement presentation.
observations.reason:
Lack of manpower to conduct physical inventory.
Ongoing physical inventory count

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that – 

The City Accountant and City Engineer work together closely to determine the identity or category of purportedly completed projects totaling P261,080,162.82 that still remained with the CIP account and effect the transfer of amount to appropriate PPE accounts.



The Accomplishment Report on Infrastructure Project and copies of Certificates of Completion and Acceptance by the City Engineer’s Office be submitted regularly to the Office of the City Accountant as basis for the preparation of the Journal Entry Voucher that will record the transfer.

We recommended that: 

The City Accountant prepare Journal Entry Voucher  (JEV) to adjust the erroneous entry in recording discounts on advance payment of real property tax intended for CY 2017 but paid in 2016.


The OIC-City Treasurer demand from the two collectors at the Land Tax Division to remit immediately the RPT collections in their possession.  Otherwise, file appropriate administrative or criminal charges against the two accountable officers. Also, he must strictly impose the daily turn-over of collections to ensure that government funds are properly protected against loss or misappropriation.

The OIC-City Treasurer must ascertain other causes for the big drop in RPT collections so that appropriate action could be implemented to avoid occurrence of the deficiency.

We recommended to the OIC-City Treasurer that–

Accounts with Land Bank of the Philippines – Trust Fund (Congressional) and Development Bank of the Philippines – NALGU pertaining to funds from the national government be deposited back to the National Treasury pursuant to COA Circular No. 2015-001 dated January 29, 2015.

Dormant accounts under the Philippine National Bank be activated first and accordingly transferred to the active account of the authorized government depository bank of the City.
observations.managementAction: Request for a passage of a resolution to the Sangguniang Panlungsod authorizing the Hon. City Mayor and the City Treasurer to close the following accounts of the City of Bago for reasons that said accounts become dormant, and return to the National Treasury for deposit.
observations.reason: Dormant accounts under the Special Education Fund and Trust Fund were already closed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the City Mayor to direct all persons liable to settle immediately the audit disallowances, or if in case aggrieved of the decision of the auditor, appeal the audit disallowances within six months period from receipt of the notice, else it shall become final and executory as prescribed under                       Section 48 and 51 of PD No. 1445.

We recommended that the City Accountant ensure that all documentary requirements for entitlement of hazard pay as stated under Section 16.1.2 of the IRR of RA 9433, and paragraph 5.8 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 be attached as supporting documents to the claim of hazard pay of Public Social Workers.

We also recommended that the management prepare a list of positions in its plantilla with the corresponding job description that describes the basis for justifying such positions that are exposed to high risk or low risk hazard.

We recommended that management follow up from COA-ICFAU the issuance of a credit advice especially that liquidation vouchers/reports for these cash advances were already submitted to the COA Chairman and effect appropriate entry to record the liquidation once the Credit Advice is received.

We recommended that the General Services Officer-Designate identify, inventory and classify all insurable assets and properties of the City and ensure that all are adequately covered/insured with the GIF of the GSIS.
observations.managementAction: To date, 17 government buildings amounting to P205,616,983.11 and a total of 139 vehicles amounting to P444,252.78 have already been insured.
observations.reason: GSO still in the process of completing the inventory count of all buildings owned by the City. After such count is completed, these properties will be processed for insurance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Government of Bago maximize the utilization of the 20% Development Fund thru full implementation of planned projects, programs and activities to ensure the attainment by the constituents of social, economic and environmental development in accordance with Joint Memorandum Circular 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011 of the DILG and DBM.
observations.managementAction: Implementation of 20% Development Fund projects are closely monitored.
observations.reason:
It is the proponent implementing agencies that prepares the timetable for the schedule of the implementation of the projects. The reasons or justifications on why the fund was not optimally utilized can be competently provided by the concerned department or office.

Reiterated in this year’s AAR.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local School Board request refund of P134,000.00 from the GF considering that said expenses are not eligible to be charged against the SEF. Otherwise, the officials held responsible for the disbursements shall be liable to pay the amount improperly charged to the fund.

We further recommended that the Local School Board strictly observe the provisions of Section 272 of the Local Government Code; Article 363 of its Implementing Rules and Regulations; and Joint Circular No. 01, s. 2017 of DepEd, DBM and DILG, dated January 19, 2017, in the utilization of the Special Education Fund to ensure the legality of the expenditures charged to SEF.

We recommended that management also include GAD programs, projects and activities for the benefit of LGU employees and personnel, especially those whose salaries are below poverty line, and design programs that will address gender issues and concerns in the workplace.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the GSO and Accounting Department exert efforts
   to update and reconcile the property records of PPE with the PPE ledger
   cards to establish the correctness of PPE account balances  amounting  to
   P804,131,872.10, and for fair financial statement presentation.

We recommended that –


   a) The City  Accountant  and  City  Engineer  work  together  closely  to
      determine the identity or category of purportedly  completed  projects
      totaling P261,080,162.82 that still remained with the CIP account  and
      effect the transfer of amount to appropriate PPE accounts.


   b) The Accomplishment Report on  Infrastructure  Project  and  copies  of
      Certificates of Completion  and  Acceptance  by  the  City  Engineer’s
      Office be submitted regularly to the Office of the City Accountant  as
      basis for the preparation of  the  Journal  Entry  Voucher  that  will
      record the transfer.

We recommended that:


   a) The City Accountant prepare Journal Entry Voucher  (JEV) to adjust the
      erroneous entry in recording discounts  on  advance  payment  of  real
      property tax intended for CY 2017 but paid in 2016.


   b) The OIC-City Treasurer demand from the two collectors at the Land  Tax
      Division to remit immediately the RPT collections in their possession.
        Otherwise,  file  appropriate  administrative  or  criminal  charges
      against the two accountable officers. Also, he  must  strictly  impose
      the daily turn-over of collections to ensure that government funds are
      properly protected against loss or misappropriation.


   c) The OIC-City Treasurer must ascertain other causes for the big drop in
      RPT collections so that appropriate action  could  be  implemented  to
      avoid occurrence of the deficiency.

We recommended to the OIC-City Treasurer that–

   a.  Accounts  with  Land  Bank  of   the   Philippines   –   Trust   Fund
      (Congressional) and  Development  Bank  of  the  Philippines  –  NALGU
      pertaining to funds from the national government be deposited back  to
      the National Treasury pursuant to  COA  Circular  No.  2015-001  dated
      January 29, 2015.


   b. Dormant accounts under the Philippine National Bank be activated first
      and accordingly transferred to the active account  of  the  authorized
      government depository bank of the City.

We recommended to the City Mayor to direct all  persons  liable  to
   settle immediately the audit disallowances, or if in  case  aggrieved  of
   the decision of the auditor, appeal the audit  disallowances  within  six
   months period from receipt of the notice, else it shall become final  and
   executory as prescribed under                       Section 48 and 51  of
   PD No. 1445.

We recommended that the City Accountant ensure that all documentary
   requirements for entitlement of hazard pay as stated under Section 16.1.2
   of the IRR of RA 9433, and paragraph 5.8 of COA Circular No. 2012-001  be
   attached as supporting documents to the claim of  hazard  pay  of  Public
   Social Workers.


        We also recommended that the management prepare a list of positions
   in its plantilla with the corresponding job  description  that  describes
   the basis for justifying such positions that are exposed to high risk  or
   low risk hazard.

We recommended that the City Accountant prepare a JEV to record the
   liquidation of confidential funds amounting to P220,000.00 .

We  recommended  that  the   General   Services   Officer-Designate
   identify, inventory and classify all insurable assets and  properties  of
   the City and ensure that all are adequately covered/insured with the  GIF
   of the GSIS.

We recommended that  the  City  Government  of  Bago  maximize  the
   utilization of the 20%  Development  Fund  thru  full  implementation  of
   planned projects, programs and activities to ensure the attainment by the
   constituents  of  social,  economic  and  environmental  development   in
   accordance with Joint Memorandum Circular 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011  of
   the DILG and DBM.

We recommended that the  Local  School  Board  request  refund  of
   P134,000.00 from the GF considering that said expenses are not  eligible
   to be charged against the SEF. Otherwise, the officials held responsible
   for the disbursements shall be  liable  to  pay  the  amount  improperly
   charged to the fund.

        We further recommended that the Local School Board strictly observe
   the provisions of Section 272 of RA 7160; Article  363  of  its  IRR  and
   Joint Circular No. 01, s. 2017 of DepEd, DBM and DILG, dated January  19,
   2017, in the utilization of  the  SEF  to  ensure  the  legality  of  the
   expenditures charged to said fund.

We recommended that management also include GAD programs,  projects
   and activities for the benefit of LGU employees and personnel, especially
   those whose salaries are below poverty line,  and design programs that
   will address gender issues and concerns in the workplace.

circulars.
